session.title: can you check their Gh issue for any similar reports

blocks.text: [Image: source: /home/demo/Library/Application Support/CleanShot/media/media_hyk0WVr6WC/CleanShot 2026-04-09 at 06.35.06@2x.png]
That's tmux's **scrollback buffer limit**. By default it's only 2000 lines. Let me check your config.

You don't have a tmux config file, so you're running with the default scrollback of **2000 lines**. That's why you hit a wall when scrolling up.

Fix it by creating `~/.tmux.conf`:

```bash
# Increase scrollback buffer (default is 2000)
set-option -g history-limit 50000
```

Then reload with `tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf` (or restart tmux). This applies to **new panes only** — existing ones keep the old limit.

50,000 lines is a good balance. You can go higher (e.g., 100000) but it uses more memory per pane.

Want me to create the config file for you?

It's using ghostty[Image #2] [Image #3]

[2 images attached]

[Image: source: /home/demo/Library/Application Support/CleanShot/media/media_9YPTeXpnMr/CleanShot 2026-04-09 at 06.37.51@2x.png]

[Image: source: /home/demo/Library/Application Support/CleanShot/media/media_73eWga1T6q/CleanShot 2026-04-09 at 06.37.56@2x.png]

I see — you're running Ghostty with `scrollback-limit = 250000`, which is plenty. But look at your first screenshot: those are **tmux tabs** at the top. When tmux is running, **tmux controls the scrollback**, not Ghostty. Ghostty's `scrollback-limit` is ignored inside tmux.

So my original answer still applies — you need to set tmux's `history-limit`. Check your tmux config:

No tmux config at all, so you're running with the default `history-limit` of **2000 lines**. That's your bottleneck.

**The fix:** Add this to `~/.tmux.conf`:

```
set-option -g history-limit 50000
```

Then inside tmux run `tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf`. New panes will get the larger buffer (existing ones keep the old 2000-line limit, so you'd need to reopen them).

Want me to create the file?
yes and configure it for me

Created `~/.tmux.conf`. Now reload it inside tmux:

```
tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf
```

Then **reopen your panes/tabs** — existing ones keep the old 2000-line buffer, only new ones get the 50,000-line limit.

What I configured:
- **50k scrollback** (was 2000)
- **Mouse support** — scroll, click panes, resize with mouse
- **Window numbering starts at 1** (not 0)
- **Fast escape time** — no delay if you use vim
- **True color** for Ghostty
try run this command now,
[Request interrupted by user]
I;m using this https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux

OK, so cmux is **not tmux** — it's a native macOS terminal app that uses **libghostty** for rendering and reads your `~/.config/ghostty/config`. The `.tmux.conf` I created is irrelevant here, let me remove it.

Since cmux reads your Ghostty config and you already have `scrollback-limit = 250000`, that should be more than enough. The cutoff you're seeing is likely one of:

1. **cmux overrides or ignores the Ghostty scrollback-limit** — it may have its own internal limit
2. **A bug in cmux** — it's a relatively new app

I'd suggest:
- Check if cmux has its own config (`cmux.json` or similar) with a scrollback setting
- File an issue at [manaflow-ai/cmux](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/issues) — this seems like a bug since your Ghostty config explicitly sets 250k lines but the scrollback is clearly cutting off much earlier
can we add more? I use Claude code for long session is 250000 still enouhg?

250,000 lines is already massive — that's roughly **5-10 hours of heavy Claude Code output**. You'd run out of memory before you'd realistically need more.

The problem isn't the limit being too low — it's that cmux likely isn't respecting it. Bumping it higher won't fix a cutoff that's happening way before 250k lines.

That said, if you want to max it out just in case, Ghostty supports up to 10 million:

Your 250k is fine. The real issue is cmux not honoring it. I'd file a bug on [manaflow-ai/cmux](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/issues) — mention that `scrollback-limit = 250000` is set in your Ghostty config but scrollback cuts off much earlier in cmux.

- Check if cmux has its own config (cmux.json or similar) with a scrollback setting

can you check for me
Found it.

No scrollback setting in `cmux.json` — it only has workspace/command configs. No scrollback option available in cmux's own config.

So cmux doesn't expose its own scrollback setting and is likely not passing through Ghostty's `scrollback-limit = 250000` from your config. This is a cmux bug/missing feature — file it on [manaflow-ai/cmux](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/issues).
can you check their Gh issue for any similar reports
There's your exact issue — **#1922**:

**Known issue — [manaflow-ai/cmux#1922](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/issues/1922).**

Key finding: Ghostty's `scrollback-limit` is in **bytes**, not lines. Your `250000` = ~250KB, which is quite small for long Claude Code sessions.

**Fix** — update your Ghostty config to a much higher value:
Changed from 250KB to **50MB**. That should handle even the longest Claude Code sessions. Only applies to **new terminal panes** — reopen your tabs to pick it up.
